CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 1 S HALE. DELAYED REPORT. SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED AT A FARMSTEAD JUST SOUTH OF HALE. SEVERAL 80+ YEAR OLD TREES WERE EITHER UPROOTED OR SNAPPED AT THE BASE. TWO MACHINE SHEDS WERE DAMAGED AS WELL. EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS THE DAMAGE WAS STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE FROM A SMALL MICROBURST. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 70-80 MPH.

*** 1 FATAL *** DELAYED REPORT. SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WINDS DOWNED A TREE OVER THE ROADWAY NEAR SW 180TH AND SW INDIANOLA ROAD SOMETIME BEFORE 5 AM FRIDAY MORNING. A DRIVER OF A PICKUP APPARENTLY DID NOT SEE THE DOWNED TREE AND DROVE INTO IT, WHICH PROVED FATAL. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
ALONG HIGHWAY 169.

SIGNIFICANT STREET FLOODING IN CITY OF AUGUSTA, CITY CLOSING ONE ROAD IN WEST SIDE OF TOWN. EL DORADO REPORTING STREET FLOODING WITH A COUPLE VEHICLES STRANDED IN HIGH WATER. EMERGENCY SERVICES DID A WATER RESCUE FOR ONE OF THE MOTORISTS.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED THAT A 5 BLOCK STRETCH IN THE CITY OF EL DORADO IS EXPERIENCING SIGNIFICANT FLOODING. FIRE DEPARTMENT IS DOING A SECOND WATER RECUSE IN EL DORADO.
REPORTED WATER OVER THE BRIDGE ON S. DOUGLAS ROAD IN SOUTH EL DORADO DUE TO CONSTANT CREEK OUT OF ITS BANKS.

REPORTS OF SEVERAL RURAL ROADS FLOODED ACROSS THE COUNTY DUE TO HEAVY RAINFALL.

FLOODING FROM WHISKEY CREEK IS EFFECTING INDENPENDENCE. SEVERAL HOMES ALONG N. 19TH AND 15TH ARE SURROUNDED BY TWO FEET OF WATER. ADDITIONAL FLOODING ON S. 19TH ALSO HAS TWO FEET OF WATER SURROUNDING HOMES.
WATER HAS RECEDED IN THE COUNTY HOWEVER A COUPLE RURAL ROADS REMAIN CLOSED DUE WASH OUT DAMAGE FROM FRIDAY MORNING HEAVY RAINFALL.
SOUTHEAST KS KDOT HAS CLOSED HIGHWAY 160 FROM HIGHWAY 99 INTERSECTION IN ELK COUNTY TO HIGHWAY 75 INTERSECTION IN NORTHWEST MONTGOMERY COUNTY DUE TO HIGH WATER FROM EARLY MORNING RAINFALL.
